sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, duration INTERVAL, custom_date DATE);
INSERT INTO test_dates (duration, custom_date) VALUES (INTERVAL '1-0' year to month, '2020-02-29');
INSERT INTO test_dates (duration, custom_date) VALUES (INTERVAL '0-1' year to month, NULL);
INSERT INTO test_dates (duration, custom_date) VALUES (INTERVAL '2-0' year to month, '2021-01-01');
SELECT id, day(duration) AS days_of_duration FROM test_dates WHERE custom_date IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, custom_date, day(duration + INTERVAL '3-0' year to month) FROM test_dates WHERE day(duration) > 0;
SELECT id, COALESCE(day(duration), 0) AS days_or_zero FROM test_dates HAVING days_or_zero > 0;
SELECT id FROM (SELECT id, day(duration) AS total_days FROM test_dates) AS subquery WHERE total_days = (SELECT MAX(day(duration)) FROM test_dates);
SELECT dt.id, dt.custom_date, day(dt.duration) AS days, dt.custom_date + INTERVAL '1-0' year to month AS new_date FROM test_dates dt WHERE day(dt.duration) < 30 ORDER BY new_date DESC;
INSERT INTO test_dates (duration, custom_date) VALUES (INTERVAL '0-2' year to month, '2022-02-28');
SELECT day((SELECT duration FROM test_dates WHERE custom_date = '2020-02-29') * (INTERVAL '1-0' year to month)) AS total_days;
SELECT id, CASE WHEN day(duration) IS NULL THEN 'Undefined' ELSE 'Defined' END AS duration_status FROM test_dates;
CREATE TABLE test_events (event_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, event_duration INTERVAL);
INSERT INTO test_events (event_duration) VALUES (INTERVAL '5-0' year to month), (INTERVAL '6-0' year to month);
SELECT te.event_id, day(te.event_duration) AS event_days FROM test_events te JOIN test_dates td ON td.id = te.event_id WHERE day(te.event_duration) > 0;
SELECT id, custom_date FROM test_dates WHERE custom_date IS NOT NULL AND day(duration) + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_events) > 2;
ALTER TABLE test_dates ADD COLUMN extended_duration INTERVAL;
UPDATE test_dates SET extended_duration = duration + INTERVAL '1-5' year to month WHERE duration IS NOT NULL;
